title: Graveyard Transfer
summary: An FTP packet capture hides the real evidence in a passive-mode data stream.
scenario: An FTP retrieval looks routine until the passive-mode data connection is reconstructed. Solvers need to identify the correct data stream, recover the transferred archive, and inspect the recovered files to obtain the flag.
learningObjectives: Map control traffic to a separate FTP data channel
Reconstruct transferred evidence from packet captures

artifacts.name: graveyard_transfer_<seed>.pcap
artifacts.purpose: Generated packet capture with control and passive-mode data streams

artifacts.name: incident_memo.txt
artifacts.purpose: Narrative note describing the suspicious transfer window

hints: The interesting bytes are probably not on port 21.
Recover the transferred file before trying to search for the flag.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the FTP story, credentials, or transferred decoy files while keeping the packet-reconstruction solve path intact.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not put the flag directly into plaintext control-channel traffic.
